title: Multi-fiber photometry in head-fixed mice during spontaneous behavior and dual-cue delay Pavlovian association
author: Safa Bouabid
materials: Conduct two distinct daily sessions during photometry recordings:

steps.title: Three weeks post-surgery, place mice on...
steps.instruction: Three weeks post-surgery, place mice on a water schedule, receiving 1mL of water/day, adjusted as necessary to maintain them at 80-85% of their initial body weight for the duration of the experiments.

steps.title: Three to four days before starting...
steps.instruction: Three to four days before starting behavior experiments and photometry recordings, habituate the mice to head-fixation on a lightweight cylindrical running wheel or on a spehirical styrofoam treadmill until they freely run and transition spontaneously between resting and running.

steps.title: Dispense water rewards (5μL/reward, (random inter-trial...
steps.instruction: Dispense water rewards (5μL/reward, (random inter-trial interval: 10-30 s) through a water spout operated by an electronically controlled solenoid valve, mounted on a post a few mm away from the mice’s mouths.

steps.title: Monitor tongue spout contacts (a proxy...
steps.instruction: Monitor tongue spout contacts (a proxy for licking) by a capacitive touch circuit connected to the spout and confirmed with live video taken from a camera positioned to capture orofacial movements.

steps.title: Measure mouse location
steps.instruction: Measure mouse location

steps.title: For the mice trained on spehirical...
steps.instruction: For the mice trained on spehirical styrofoam treadmill, measure the ball rotation in pitch, yaw, and roll directions using optical computer mice through an acquisition board .

steps.title: Session 1
steps.instruction: Session 1: Perform photometry recordings while mice are spontaneously behaving on the wheel with no lick spout present and no reward available to capture purely spontaneous behavior.

steps.title: Session 2
steps.instruction: Session 2: Provide access to the lick spout and deliver unpredicted water rewards (5μL) at random intervals (10-30s).

steps.title: For unilateral optogenetic stimulation of DA...
steps.instruction: For unilateral optogenetic stimulation of DA neurons with simultaneous DA and ACh release dynamics measurements during unpredicted reward sessions, connect implanted optical fibers to a 450 nm laser diode light source (Doric).

steps.title: Use a 100 μm patch cord...
steps.instruction: Use a 100 μm patch cord (Doric) with zirconia sleeves to connect fibers to the light source.

steps.title: Calibrate laser power at the tip...
steps.instruction: Calibrate laser power at the tip of optical fibers to 2.5 mW.

steps.title: Deliver 4 ms blue light pulses...
steps.instruction: Deliver 4 ms blue light pulses at 30 Hz for 500 ms to activate dopamine neurons.

steps.title: Tigger 5 stimulation trains each session...
steps.instruction: Tigger 5 stimulation trains each session at randomized intervals (outside unpredicted reward deliveries).

steps.title: In each session (daily) present 60...
steps.instruction: In each session (daily) present 60 cue presentations (30 light and 30 tone) in a pseudorandom order.

steps.title: Deliver light cues via a 7...
steps.instruction: Deliver light cues via a 7 mW LED positioned 20 cm from the mouse and present tone cues (12 kHz, 80 dB) through a USB speaker placed 30 cm away.

steps.title: Present each cue for a duration...
steps.instruction: Present each cue for a duration of 6 seconds with water reward delivery (5μL) 3 seconds after cue onset.

steps.title: Randomly draw an inter-trial interval (ITI;...
steps.instruction: Randomly draw an inter-trial interval (ITI; time between separate trials) from a uniform distribution of 4-40s.

steps.title: Include eight additional non-contingent rewards per...
steps.instruction: Include eight additional non-contingent rewards per session during the inter-trial interval (ITI; time between separate trials) periods.

steps.title: Continue training for 26-38 days until...
steps.instruction: Continue training for 26-38 days until mice learn to associate both cues with rewards.

steps.title: After conditioning is complete, begin the partial extinction phase
steps.instruction: After conditioning is complete, begin the partial extinction phase.

steps.title: Downshift the reward probability for one...
steps.instruction: Downshift the reward probability for one cue to 20% while maintaining the other cue at 80%.

steps.title: Continue training until significant reductions in...
steps.instruction: Continue training until significant reductions in responses to the 20% cue are observed.

steps.title: Reverse the cue probabilities (20% ,...
steps.instruction: Reverse the cue probabilities (20% , 80%), counterbalancing the order across mice.

steps.title: As mice perform the behavioral tasks...
steps.instruction: As mice perform the behavioral tasks (spontaneous behavior, reward sessions, optogenetic stimulation, or Pavlovian conditioning), conduct fluorescence measurements from the multi-fiber arrays using a custom-built microscope setup mounted on a 4’ W x 8’ L x 12’ thick vibration isolation table.

steps.title: Filter and focus emission light using...
steps.instruction: Filter and focus emission light using a bandpass filter and a tube lens onto the CMOS sensor of the Hamamatsu camera, creating an image of the fiber bundle.

steps.title: To enable precise manual focusing, connect...
steps.instruction: To enable precise manual focusing, connect microscope to a micromanipulator and mount on a rotatable arm extending over the head-fixation setup to facilitate positioning of the objective above the imaging surface over the mouse head.

steps.title: Open the basic imaging application HCImage...
steps.instruction: Open the basic imaging application HCImage Live (Hamamatsu) that is included with the Hamamatsu camera.

steps.title: Perform dual wavelength excitation in a...
steps.instruction: Perform dual wavelength excitation in a quasi-simultaneous externally triggered imaging mode.

steps.title: Alternate 470 nm excitation with 570...
steps.instruction: Alternate 470 nm excitation with 570 nm excitation at 36 Hz with 20 ms exposure time, to achieve a frame rate of 18 Hz for each excitation wavelength, respectively.

steps.title: Control the timing and duration of...
steps.instruction: Control the timing and duration of TTL pulses through a custom MATLAB software and a programmable digital acquisition card (NIDAQ, National Instruments PCIe 6343).

steps.title: Transmit voltage pulses to the NIDAQ...
steps.instruction: Transmit voltage pulses to the NIDAQ from the camera following the exposure of each frame to confirm proper camera triggering and to synchronize imaging data with behavioral data.

steps.title: Record videos of the fiber bundle...
steps.instruction: Record videos of the fiber bundle surface throughout the behavioral session.

steps.title: Apply motion correction to the acquired...
steps.instruction: Apply motion correction to the acquired videos using a whole-frame cross-correlation algorithm ( Dombeck et al. , 2010 ; Miri et al. , 2011 ).

steps.title: Extract fluorescence changes from each fiber...
steps.instruction: Extract fluorescence changes from each fiber using previously described methods ( Vu et al. , 2024 ). See Github repositories: BouabidVu2026 and MultifiberProcessing

steps.title: Extract mean fluorescence time series from each region of interest
steps.instruction: Extract mean fluorescence time series from each region of interest.

steps.title: Fit a 2-term exponential function to...
steps.instruction: Fit a 2-term exponential function to the mean fluorescence time series to define the baseline.

steps.title: Remove low-frequency artifacts by high-pass filtering...
steps.instruction: Remove low-frequency artifacts by high-pass filtering the ΔF/F signals using a finite impulse response filter.

steps.title: For green sensors (ACh or other...
steps.instruction: For green sensors (ACh or other green fluorophores), set the passband frequency at 0.3 Hz, for red sensors (dopamine or other red fluorophores), set the passband frequency at 0.1 Hz. For experiments involving optogenetic stimulation of midbrain DA neurons, skip high-pass filtering to preserve longer timescale dynamics of stimulation effects on ACh signals.

steps.title: Calculate ΔF/F signals by Normalize fluorescence signals to baselinn
steps.instruction: Calculate ΔF/F signals by Normalize fluorescence signals to baselinn.

steps.title: For the mice trained on lightweight...
steps.instruction: For the mice trained on lightweight cylindrical running wheel, sample the wheel's linear velocity at 2Khz using a rotary encoder (E2-5000, US Digital) attached to the wheel axle.
